GS3.trivia: The first successful suborbital flight test of ISRO's RLV-TD was conducted on May 23, 2016.

GS3.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Reforms for Disaster Management Framework, Emphasizes Community Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to India's disaster management framework.
• The report emphasized a shift from a reactive, relief-centric approach to a proactive, prevention, and mitigation-focused strategy.
• Key recommendations include strengthening early warning systems, enhancing inter-agency coordination, and investing in disaster-resilient infrastructure.
• A strong focus was placed on building community resilience through local capacity building, awareness programs, and integration of traditional knowledge.
• The proposed reforms aim to make India more prepared to handle the increasing frequency and intensity of natural disasters exacerbated by climate change.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Committee recommended reforms for disaster management.
Shift from reactive to proactive, prevention-focused approach.
Key recommendations: early warning, inter-agency coordination, resilient infrastructure.
Emphasizes community resilience and local capacity building.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the evolution of India's disaster management framework since the Disaster Management Act, 2005, and discuss the areas requiring further reform.
Discuss the importance of community participation and local capacity building in building disaster resilience, providing examples from India.
Examine how climate change is exacerbating natural disasters in India and what policy measures are needed to integrate climate change adaptation into disaster management strategies.
GS3.imageDescription: A rescue team evacuating people during a flood, symbolizing disaster management.
GS3.trivia: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) was established under the Disaster Management Act, 2005, with the Prime Minister as its Chairperson.

GS3.headline: India Participates in Multi-National Naval Exercise in Indian Ocean Region, Enhances Maritime Security Cooperation
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy actively participated in a multi-national naval exercise conducted in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR), alongside several friendly navies.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, sharing best practices in maritime security, and strengthening regional cooperation against common threats.
• Drills included anti-submarine warfare, anti-piracy operations, search and rescue missions, and communication exercises.
• India's participation underscores its growing role as a net security provider in the IOR and its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Such exercises are crucial for maintaining maritime domain awareness, deterring illegal activities, and ensuring stability in a strategically vital region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
India and its neighborhood- relations
prelimsPoints: Indian Navy participated in a multi-national naval exercise in IOR.
Focus on interoperability, maritime security, regional cooperation.
Drills included anti-submarine warfare, anti-piracy, search and rescue.
India acts as a net security provider in the IOR.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategic imperatives and evolving role in ensuring maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region, citing its participation in multi-national naval exercises.
Analyze the challenges to maritime security in the Indo-Pacific and how collaborative efforts like naval exercises contribute to addressing these threats.
Examine the concept of India as a 'net security provider' in the IOR and its implications for regional stability and India's foreign policy objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian naval ships participating in a joint exercise at sea.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans, covering about 20% of the water on Earth's surface.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates New Megawatt-Scale Solar Power Park in Rajasthan, Boosts Renewable Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• India inaugurated a new multi-megawatt solar power park in Rajasthan, significantly adding to the nation's renewable energy capacity.
• The park, developed through a public-private partnership, utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology to maximize energy generation efficiency.
• Rajasthan's abundant solar irradiance and vast desert lands make it an ideal location for large-scale solar projects.
• This expansion is a crucial step towards India's ambitious target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030 and reducing its carbon footprint.
• The project also contributes to local employment generation and promotes sustainable development in the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: New multi-megawatt solar power park inaugurated in Rajasthan.
Adds to India's renewable energy capacity.
Rajasthan ideal for solar projects due to high solar irradiance.
Contributes to 500 GW renewable energy target by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential and challenges of solar energy development in India, particularly in states like Rajasthan, and its role in achieving national renewable energy targets.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of investing in large-scale renewable energy projects for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships (PPPs) in accelerating the deployment of renewable energy infrastructure in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Rows of solar panels stretching across a desert landscape under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India has the fourth-largest installed renewable energy capacity in the world.

GS3.headline: India's Indigenous Aircraft Carrier Conducts Advanced Sea Trials, Nearing Operational Deployment
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier successfully completed a series of advanced sea trials, moving closer to its full operational deployment.
• The trials tested the carrier's propulsion systems, aviation facilities, weapon systems, and overall combat readiness.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's quest for self-reliance in defence manufacturing and enhances its blue-water naval capabilities.
• The carrier is expected to significantly boost the Indian Navy's prowess and project India's power and influence across the Indian Ocean Region.
• Its operationalization will provide a robust platform for air dominance and maritime surveillance, strengthening national security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: India's first indigenous aircraft carrier completed advanced sea trials.
Tested propulsion, aviation, weapon systems.
Significant for self-reliance in defence manufacturing.
Boosts Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous aircraft carriers for India's naval power projection and its role in securing national interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the progress and challenges in achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing in India, with specific reference to naval shipbuilding.
Examine how India's growing blue-water naval capabilities contribute to regional stability and its broader foreign policy objectives in the Indo-Pacific.
GS3.imageDescription: A large aircraft carrier sailing on the ocean with fighter jets on its deck.
GS3.trivia: An aircraft carrier is a warship that serves as a seagoing airbase, equipped with a full-length flight deck and facilities for carrying, arming, deploying, and recovering aircraft.
